text: We always appreciate varied perspectives here: cross pollination.

elements.text: We always appreciate varied perspectives here: cross pollination.

text: Hi, I'm Alex, I'm based in Toronto and currently assisting on a research/coding language project about computation history, specifically the work of Seymour Papert (if you have LOGO memories, I'm always happy to chat!) and Joseph Weizenbaum. I recently completed a Masters at U of T and am doing software-studies-slash-media-philosophy-type-stuff in my own research, but I come from a humanities background and am finding my footing in code. I just applied to PhD programs this Fall with a project on object orientation, French philosophy, and virtual pets; though as i get increasingly into weirder and weirder computation projects and weirder and weirder philosophy I fear it may spin into something much less intelligible :sweat_smile:. I've been really enjoying exploring the different things computing could have been (and still can be), and the podcast has been a great fit for that!
